# Produce Storage / Market Aggregator Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-produce-aggregator-produce-aggregator-site
**Vertical slug:** produce-aggregator
**VN-ID:** VN-AGR-MKT-002
**Niche family:** NF-AGR-MKT (standalone agricultural market)
**Family role:** standalone
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Produce aggregators in Nigeria bridge the gap between smallholder farmers and buyers (supermarkets, exporters, food processors, urban markets). Key platforms and models include AFEX Commodities Exchange, NIRSAL Microfinance Bank's farmer aggregation scheme, and cooperative-based aggregators across Benue (yam, cassava), Kebbi (rice), and Plateau (vegetables, potatoes). Cold storage and post-harvest loss reduction (Nigeria loses ~40% of produce post-harvest) are critical service differentiators. WhatsApp commodity price updates, SMS alerts, and bulk buyer negotiation via phone are the primary business communication channels.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Smallholder farmer Bello, 45, Benue State**: Produces 10 tonnes of yam per season. Wants guaranteed offtake price and collection from farm gate. Needs trusted aggregator with AFEX registration.
- **Supermarket buyer Chioma, 38, Lagos**: Sources fresh vegetables (tomatoes, peppers, carrots) and tubers weekly. Needs consistent quality, pre-graded produce, and reliable delivery.
- **Export company Mr Abubakar, 52, Kano**: Aggregates sesame, groundnut, and soybean for international buyers. Needs FMARD certification, fumigation records, and phytosanitary certificate.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home**: Hero (aggregator name, "Connecting Farmers to Markets"), WhatsApp offtake enquiry CTA, current commodity price display (₦/kg), featured produce (with grading specs), trust strip (AFEX, NIRSAL, FMARD, CAC)
- **About**: Company background, farming communities served, storage capacity, AFEX/NIRSAL partnership, export capability
- **Commodities**: Produce catalogue with grade specs, ₦/tonne pricing, availability calendar, MOQ for bulk buyers
- **Contact**: WhatsApp enquiry (primary), office address, farmer registration form (NDPR-compliant)

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Agricultural, trustworthy, and market-connecting. "Fair Price. Guaranteed Offtake", "From Farm Gate to Market", "AFEX Registered Aggregator". Primary CTA: "Enquire About Offtake via WhatsApp". Secondary: "Register as a Farmer Partner". Avoid: western agricultural stock imagery.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- AFEX Commodities Exchange registration
- NIRSAL partnership or accreditation
- FMARD certification
- CAC business registration
- Phytosanitary certificate (for export produce)
- Fumigation compliance records
- Farmer cooperative partnerships (named)

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Farm gate collection — aggregator truck at Benue yam farm, farmers loading, rural Nigerian setting
2. Produce grading — workers sorting tomatoes/peppers in shed, quality control
3. Storage facility — well-ventilated warehouse, stacked produce sacks, AFEX branding
4. Commodity price board — ₦/kg pricing display, Nigerian market context
5. Farmer registration day — cooperative meeting, AFEX field officer with farmers, Plateau State
6. Export packaging — produce in export-standard boxes with phytosanitary stickers
7. Market delivery — truck delivering to Lagos Mile 12 or Onitsha main market

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- [ ] AFEX Commodities Exchange membership
- [ ] NIRSAL accreditation (if applicable)
- [ ] FMARD aggregation licence
- [ ] CAC business registration
- [ ] Phytosanitary certificate for export commodities
- [ ] NDPR consent on farmer registration form

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp offtake enquiry CTA prominent
- [x] AFEX/NIRSAL/FMARD trust signals
- [x] Nigerian produce referenced (yam, cassava, tomatoes, sesame, groundnut)
- [x] ₦/kg commodity pricing
- [x] Nigerian farming region references (Benue, Kebbi, Plateau)
- [x] Mobile-first layout
- [x] esc() on all inputs
- [x] NDPR on farmer registration form
